SWAT Leadership Guide

swat-leaders
  1. Quick Start
  2. Home & Admin Navigation
  3. Nominations & Approvals
  4. Volunteer Management
  5. SWAT Reports
  6. Events & Serve Days
  7. Volunteer Notifications
  8. Captain Management
  9. Settings & Datastore

Quick Start

SWAT Leadership oversees the entire Waterboyz project lifecycle — reviewing new nominations, approving budgets, assigning Project Captains, coordinating volunteers, and reporting results back to the community. This guide walks through the admin pages you'll use most.

How to get there

Sign in at waterboyz.kinops.io. If your account is on the SWAT Leadership team, the hamburger menu will expose an Admin section with every management page listed below.

First: check your home page

When you log in, your home page shows My Nominations, the Nominate a Family programs, and a My Tasks sidebar with approvals assigned to you or the Leadership team. Clear any pending approvals here before moving on.

Second: review new nominations

Open the My Tasks sidebar or the Actions page to find swat-project-approval tasks. Review each nomination, set an approved budget, and assign an initial Project Captain so the project can move into the swat-projects datastore.

Third: manage volunteers and events

Use Admin → Volunteer Management to search the volunteer directory, and Events to create serve days and assign signed-up volunteers to projects. Both pages are where day-to-day coordination happens.

Fourth: report out

Open Admin → SWAT Reports for live totals (projects, man hours, counties served) with filters for grant applications. Use Ctrl+P to export a clean printable summary.
Tips
  • Leadership tasks go to the entire Leadership team by default — coordinate with the rest of the team so the same approval isn't worked twice.
  • Most admin pages have a <strong>Refresh</strong> button in the toolbar. If you just changed something in another tab, click it instead of reloading the whole page.
  • If a page looks empty or you see a "Not authorized" redirect, double-check that your account still has the <strong>SWAT Leadership</strong> team membership.

Home & Admin Navigation

The SWAT Leadership home page is the daily starting point — it shows your open approvals, your own nominations, and quick links into every admin tool. The Admin landing page (reached through the menu) is the directory for everything else.

How to get there

Click the Waterboyz logo or Home to return to the dashboard. Open the hamburger menu (top-right) and choose Admin to see every management tool in one place.

The hero banner

The top of the home page shows your display name with the SWAT Leadership eyebrow label, confirming you're logged in with leadership permissions. If the eyebrow is missing or says something else, your role may not be set up correctly.

My Nominations panel

The left column lists your three most recent nominations across both swat-project-nomination and christmas-alive-family-nomination. Click any row to open the nomination, or View all to go to the full Nominations page.

Nominate a Family section

Below your nominations is a card for each active program (SWAT Projects, Christmas Alive, and any others configured in the programs datastore). Click a card to start a new nomination on behalf of a family or partner.

My Tasks sidebar

The right column shows open Approval and Task submissions assigned to you or to a team you belong to (including the SWAT Leadership team). New swat-project-approval items land here for review.

The Admin menu

Open the hamburger menu → Admin. This page lists every leadership tool as a card: SWAT Reports, Volunteer Management, Volunteer Notifications, Captain Management, plus any admin forms (such as Events) configured for the kapp. Space Admins also see a Datastore card.
Tips
  • The hamburger menu is always visible in the top nav bar on both desktop and mobile — you never lose access to the admin pages.
  • Mobile users also get a bottom nav bar for quick access to Home, My Volunteering, My Nominations, and Projects.
  • The <strong>Admin</strong> card page is simpler than the menu because it also surfaces any dynamic admin forms (like <strong>Events</strong>) that aren't pinned in the navigation.

Nominations & Approvals

Every SWAT project starts life as a nomination submitted through a service request form. SWAT Leadership reviews each nomination, confirms the family details, sets an approved budget, and either approves or denies the work.

How to get there

Open a nomination from your My Tasks sidebar, from My Nominations, or by going directly to /actions to see every approval task assigned to you or the Leadership team.

How nominations arrive

Churches, community partners, and individuals submit the public swat-project-nomination form (or the seasonal christmas-alive-family-nomination). Each submission creates a Nominations-type record that enters the approval queue automatically.

Opening an approval task

In the My Tasks list on the home page — or at /actions — click the swat-project-approval item. The form opens with the original nomination details on top and a Leadership review section below.

Reviewing the family details

Confirm the family name, address, county, family type, and the scope of work described by the nominator. Reach out to the nominator directly if anything is missing or unclear before making a decision.

Setting the budget and captain

Enter an Approved Budget and pick an Initial Project Captain from the dropdown (sourced from the SWAT Project Captains team). Approving the request creates a record in the swat-projects datastore and notifies the captain.

Tracking your own nominations

If you submitted a nomination yourself, it appears in My Nominations on the home page. Click through to see its current approval state — Draft, Submitted, Approved, or Denied.

Field reference

Approved Budget
Dollar amount authorized for the project. Used later for expense tracking and reimbursements.
Initial Project Captain
The captain who will receive the project and begin coordinating volunteers. Can be changed later from Project Details.
Family Type
One or more of Foster Family, Special Needs Family, Single Parent Family, Senior Citizen, or Other. Drives SWAT Reports breakdowns.
County
The county where the work will happen. Used in reporting and for matching volunteers by Preferred Service Area.
Tips
  • Leadership approvals are routed to the whole team — if someone else picks one up first, you'll see it drop off your task list. Communicate so the same nomination isn't reviewed twice.
  • Even if you deny a nomination, the record stays in the system so you can explain the decision later or refer the family to another program.

Volunteer Management

The Volunteer Management admin page is a spreadsheet-style view of every record in the <code>volunteers</code> datastore. Use it to search, filter, edit, and connect volunteers to projects and events.

How to get there

Open the hamburger menu → Admin → Volunteer Management (or go directly to /admin/volunteer-management).

Searching and filtering

The global search box at the top matches across first name, last name, email, phone, skills, tools, languages, organization, and location. For narrower filters, open the Filters toggle and use the inline filter row at the top of each column — text columns take a search term, array/enum columns (Skills, Tools, Languages, Service Area, Org, City, State, Availability) get a multi-select dropdown.

Choosing which columns to show

Click the Columns button to toggle columns on and off. Address, Zip, Other Skills, Bio, Availability, Other Availability, Dietary Restrictions, Photo Consent, and Username are hidden by default; turn them on when you need them. The table scrolls horizontally when many columns are visible.

Opening a volunteer

Click any row (or any card on mobile) to open the volunteer detail drawer on the right. The drawer has three tabs: Profile (read-only overview grouped into Location & Languages, Skills & Tools, Availability, Preferences, and Bio), Events, and Projects.

Assigning to projects or events

On the Projects tab, use the Assign to Project picker to add the volunteer to an active project — this creates a record in swat-project-volunteers. On the Events tab, use Sign Up for Event to enroll them in any Open or Planning event. Remove assignments or cancel signups with the trash icon; the original record stays in place as history.

Editing or adding a volunteer

Click the pencil icon in the drawer header to open the full volunteers form — including the CategoryPicker widgets for Skill Areas and Tools — and edit any field. From the page toolbar, the Add Volunteer button launches the same form in create mode so you can register a volunteer from an email or phone call.

Field reference

First Name / Last Name
Primary identity. Shown in every view and used for name search.
Email Address / Phone Number
Contact fields. Email is clickable (opens a mailto link); phone is formatted for display.
Affiliated Organization
The church, employer, or community org the volunteer serves with. Sourced from the <code>affiliates</code> datastore.
Skill Areas
Multi-select categorized skills from the <code>skills</code> datastore. Drives Skills filter and notification targeting.
Tools
Multi-select categorized tools from the <code>tools</code> datastore. Useful when staffing projects that need specific equipment.
Languages You Know
Languages the volunteer can communicate in. Important when matching to families with limited English.
Preferred Service Area
Counties the volunteer is willing to travel to. Filters projects and notifications geographically.
How often can you volunteer
Availability cadence (weekly, monthly, occasional, etc.).
Photo Consent
Yes or No — indicates whether the volunteer consents to being photographed at serve days.
Projects / Events counts
Live counts of linked records from <code>swat-project-volunteers</code> and the Event Sign Up submissions.
Tips
  • Removing a project assignment is a soft delete — the <code>swat-project-volunteers</code> record's Status is set to <em>Removed</em>, so you can see the history and re-activate it later.
  • On mobile the table collapses to a card list. Tap a card to open the same detail drawer used on desktop.
  • The Add Volunteer form is the same Kinetic form volunteers fill out themselves, so all required fields and validation still apply.

SWAT Reports

The SWAT Reports page is a live dashboard over every record in <code>swat-projects</code>. Use it to answer grant-writing questions, track man hours, and make quick data corrections without opening each project.

How to get there

Open the hamburger menu → Admin → SWAT Reports (or go directly to /admin/reports).

Summary cards

Four cards at the top show Total Projects, Total Man Hours, Completed, and Counties Served. The numbers update as you apply filters, so you can see stats for a single county or time window.

Breakdown charts

Three horizontal bar charts split the current project set by County, Family Type, and Status. Counts and percentages update live with your filters.

Filtering projects

The Filter Projects panel has six controls: Search (name, county, captain), County, Family Type, Status, Completed / Scheduled After, and Completed / Scheduled Before. Click Clear all to reset. Date filters use whichever of Completion Date or Scheduled Date is populated.

Inline editing

Click any Status, Hours, or Family Type cell to edit it directly in the table. Status is a dropdown (Planning, Ready to Work, Active, Ongoing, Completed, Canceled), Hours is a number input, and Family Type is a multi-select checkbox list. Changes save immediately with an optimistic UI update.

Opening a project

Click the project name to open the full project detail page. The back button returns you to SWAT Reports with your filters intact, so you don't lose your place.

Printing or exporting

Use Ctrl+P (or Cmd+P) to generate a clean printable copy of the filtered dashboard — useful for grant applications and quarterly board reports.

Field reference

Project Status
Planning, Ready to Work, Active, Ongoing, Completed, or Canceled. Drives the Status breakdown and the completed-count card.
Total Project Man Hours
Total volunteer hours logged on the project. Sums into the Man Hours card and is editable inline.
Family Type
Stored as a JSON array on each project. One or more of Foster Family, Special Needs Family, Single Parent Family, Senior Citizen, Other.
County
County where the project is located. Drives the County breakdown and County filter.
Scheduled Date / Completion Date
Date filters use whichever of these two fields is populated, preferring Completion Date for finished projects.
Tips
  • Inline edits only apply to the fields exposed on this page — for anything else open the project and edit it there.
  • Reports fetches up to 1,000 projects in a single call, so it stays fast. If you ever hit that cap, tighten the date range before filtering further.
  • Printing respects your current filters — set up the view you want, then print.
